title: hyprwhspr configuration
description: Configuration schema for hyprwhspr speech-to-text system

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for IDE autocompletion and validation

primary_shortcut.description: Primary keyboard shortcut to trigger recording (e.g., 'SUPER+ALT+D')

secondary_shortcut.description: Optional secondary hotkey for language-specific recording (e.g., 'SUPER+ALT+I')

cancel_shortcut.description: Optional shortcut to cancel recording and discard audio without transcribing (e.g., 'SUPER+ESCAPE')

recording_mode.description: Recording mode: 'toggle' (press to start/stop), 'push_to_talk' (hold to record), 'auto' (hybrid tap/hold), 'continuous' (keeps transcribing, auto-pastes on speech pause), 'long_form' (extended recording with pause/resume; requires long_form_submit_shortcut)

grab_keys.description: Exclusive keyboard grab. False is safer; true suppresses shortcut from other apps. Disable for compatibility with keyboard remappers (keyd, kmonad).

use_hypr_bindings.description: Use Hyprland compositor bindings instead of evdev (disables GlobalShortcuts)

selected_device_name.description: Specific keyboard device name (e.g., 'USB Keyboard'). Takes priority over path if both set.

keyboard_hotplug.description: Watch for keyboards plugged in after startup (pyudev) and attach them live, so docked/Bluetooth/USB keyboards work without restarting the service. True (default) enables this for any keyboard that passes the same checks as startup discovery (must emit every shortcut key). Set false to restore startup-only discovery.

keyboard_device_names.description: Optional allowlist of keyboard device names. When set, only listed devices are grabbed at startup AND on hotplug. Ignored if selected_device_name or selected_device_path is set. Null (default) = no name filter.

audio_device_id.description: PortAudio device index, or a PulseAudio/PipeWire source name (from `pactl list short sources`). The index can change on reboot; the source name is matched against PortAudio devices.

audio_device_name.description: PortAudio/PulseAudio device-name substring (more stable across reboots). A PulseAudio/PipeWire source name is also accepted.

audio_device_vendor_id.description: USB vendor ID for audio device (most stable identifier, from udev)

keepalive_stream.description: Hold a silent input stream open between recordings so the mic stays warm (fixes 'paTimedOut' on some USB mics). Off by default; when on, desktops show the mic-in-use indicator continuously.

audio_device_model_id.description: USB model ID for audio device (most stable identifier, from udev)

model.description: Whisper model to use for local transcription (e.g., 'base', 'base.en', 'small', 'medium', 'large-v3')

threads.description: Thread count for whisper processing; omit for auto = min(8, CPU count)

language.description: Language code for transcription (null = auto-detect, or 'en', 'nl', 'fr', etc.)

word_overrides.description: Dictionary of word replacements applied after transcription: {"original": "replacement"}

filter_filler_words.description: Remove common filler words (uh, um, er, etc.) from transcription output

filler_words.description: Filler words to remove when filter_filler_words is enabled

whisper_prompt.default: Transcribe with proper capitalization, including sentence beginnings, proper nouns, titles, and standard English capitalization rules.
whisper_prompt.description: Prompt passed to Whisper to guide transcription style

task.description: 'transcribe' outputs the source language; 'translate' translates speech into English. Supported by pywhispercpp and faster-whisper.

sampling_strategy.description: Whisper decoding strategy (pywhispercpp, faster-whisper). 'beam_search' tracks multiple candidates for better accuracy; 'greedy' is faster but lower quality. Locked at model load for pywhispercpp (restart to change).

beam_size.description: Number of candidate sequences tracked during beam search (beam_search only). Higher is more accurate but slower.

post_transcription_hook.description: Shell command run after preprocessing, before paste. Stdin receives the transcription; non-empty stdout replaces it. Empty stdout = observer-only (original text is kept). Env vars HYPRWHSPR_MODEL and HYPRWHSPR_BACKEND are set. Subject to a 5s timeout; errors pass through the original text.

clipboard_behavior.description: True = clear clipboard after delay, false = keep clipboard contents

clipboard_clear_delay.description: Seconds to wait before clearing clipboard (only used if clipboard_behavior is true)

paste_keycode_wev.description: Wayland/XKB keycode as printed by wev for the key that types 'v'. Converted to Linux evdev by subtracting 8.

paste_keycode.description: Linux evdev keycode for the paste key. Default 47 = KEY_V (QWERTY). Set to the keycode for the physical key that produces 'v' on your layout.

applications.description: Per-application injection behavior keyed by normalized focused-window identifiers. Set auto_paste to a key chord like 'ctrl+y', or false to disable injection entirely for that app (nothing pasted, clipboard left untouched).

oneOf.description: Paste key chord to send for this app, e.g. 'ctrl+y' or 'ctrl+shift+v'.
Disable injection while this app is focused: nothing is pasted and the clipboard is left untouched.

shift_paste.description: Back-compat: true = Ctrl+Shift+V, false = Ctrl+V. Only used if paste_mode is absent.

prefer_clipboard_paste.description: Force verbatim clipboard paste instead of direct typing on GNOME/Mutter. Direct typing (ydotool type) is only correct for ASCII text on a US layout; hyprwhspr already switches to clipboard paste automatically for non-US layouts or non-ASCII text, so set this only to force clipboard paste in all cases.

rest_endpoint_url.description: Full HTTP or HTTPS URL for remote transcription endpoint

rest_api_key.description: DEPRECATED: API key for authentication. Use credential manager instead.

rest_headers.description: Additional HTTP headers for remote transcription

rest_body.description: Additional body fields for remote transcription

rest_timeout.description: Request timeout in seconds for REST API calls

websocket_provider.description: Provider identifier for WebSocket credential lookup (e.g., 'openai', 'google', 'elevenlabs')

websocket_url.description: Explicit WebSocket URL (auto-derived from provider if null)

realtime_timeout.description: Completion timeout in seconds for WebSocket transcription

realtime_buffer_max_seconds.description: Max buffer in seconds before dropping audio chunks

realtime_transcription_delay.description: Latency/accuracy delay setting for OpenAI gpt-realtime-whisper transcription

pywhispercpp_use_vad.description: Enable native Silero VAD for the pywhispercpp backend (strips silence, reduces hallucination loops). Auto-downloads the ~1MB ggml-silero VAD model on first use.

onnx_asr_quantization.description: Quantization mode for ONNX ASR. 'int8' for CPU performance, null for fp32.

onnx_asr_use_vad.description: Use Voice Activity Detection for recordings at or above onnx_asr_vad_min_duration

onnx_asr_vad_min_duration.description: Minimum recording duration in seconds before ONNX ASR routes audio through VAD

faster_whisper_device.description: 'auto' detects CUDA; falls back to CPU. Set explicitly if needed.

faster_whisper_compute_type.description: 'auto' uses int8 on CUDA, float32 on CPU. Set to 'int8' explicitly on CPU for faster inference at some accuracy cost.

faster_whisper_vad_filter.description: Enable built-in Silero VAD to strip silence and reduce hallucination loops

cohere_transcribe_device.description: Device for the Cohere Transcribe backend. 'auto' detects CUDA and falls back to CPU.

cohere_transcribe_dtype.description: Compute dtype for Cohere Transcribe. GPU runs bfloat16 (the model's native precision); true float16 is not usable as it overflows the attention mask, so a 'float16' setting is treated as bfloat16. 'float32' forces full precision and is used on CPU.

cohere_transcribe_compile.description: Enable torch.compile for Cohere Transcribe (faster throughput; adds warmup on first call).

start_sound_volume.description: Volume multiplier for recording start sound

stop_sound_volume.description: Volume multiplier for recording stop sound

start_sound_path.description: Custom path for start sound (null = built-in ping-up.ogg)

stop_sound_path.description: Custom path for stop sound (null = built-in ping-down.ogg)

error_sound_path.description: Custom path for error sound (null = built-in ping-error.ogg)

mic_osd_enabled.description: Show recording status during recording. Shown as the animated overlay on layer-shell compositors (Hyprland, Sway, niri, KDE), or as desktop notifications on GNOME/Mutter (which has no layer-shell support).

notification_timeout_ms.description: How long non-critical desktop notifications stay on screen, in milliseconds. These are also marked transient so they never pile up in the notification center. Critical errors ignore this and persist until dismissed. Note: GNOME Shell ignores notification timeouts and uses its own banner duration.

mute_detection.description: Enable mute detection to cancel recording when mic is muted

audio_ducking_percent.description: How much to reduce volume BY (50 = reduce to 50% of original)

auto_submit.description: Send Enter key after pasting text (for chat/search inputs)

long_form_temp_limit_mb.description: Max temp storage in MB for long-form segments

long_form_auto_save_interval.description: Auto-save interval in seconds for long-form recording (default: 5 minutes)

continuous_silence_seconds.description: Seconds of silence before auto-pasting transcription in continuous mode

continuous_silence_threshold.description: RMS level below which audio is considered silence in continuous mode. 0 (default) = auto-calibrate from noise floor at session start. Set manually if auto-calibration misbehaves on your mic.

silence_timeout.description: Auto-stop recording after this many seconds of silence in toggle/auto modes. 0 (default) = disabled. The timer only arms after speech is detected, so it won't fire while you compose your first sentence.
